author: linghengqian
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...]

- No.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

- Add Windows installation packages in `.msixbundle` format.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

- Null.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

- Based on the actions described in https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/discussions/27292 , Microsoft has decided that the `.msixbundle` format will replace the `.exe` and `.msi` formats for installers.

title: DBeaver CE Dump Fails when Workbench export/import works
author: Linda-A-LF
body: ### Description I attempted to dump a MySQL database using the DBeaver v26.1.2 software. The MySQL dump progress log had these entries : 2026-07-10 12:53:25.875 - Dump database into file:///C:/Exports/10.101.3.188_dump-forms-202607101253.sql C:\Users\lilorent\AppData\Roaming\DBeaverData\drivers\clients\mysql_8\win\mysqldump.exe --single-transaction --routines --add-drop-table --disable-keys --extended-insert -u MyChief --host=10.101.3.188 --port=7325 forms Task 'MySQL dump' started at Fri Jul 10 12:53:25 HST 2026 mysqldump: Error in field count for table: `backupipu_bus` ! Aborting. Task 'MySQL dump' finished at Fri Jul 10 12:53:30 HST 2026 2026-07-10 12:53:31.006 - IO error: Process failed (exit code = 3). See error log. 2026-07-10 12:53:31.006 - java.io.IOException: Process failed (exit code = 3). See error log. at org.jkiss.dbeaver.tasks.nativetool.AbstractNativeToolHandler.validateErrorCode(AbstractNativeToolHandler.java:264) at org.jkiss.dbeaver.tasks.nativetool.AbstractNativeToolHandler.executeProcess(AbstractNativeToolHandler.java:242) at org.jkiss.dbeaver.tasks.nativetool.AbstractNativeToolHandler.doExecute(AbstractNativeToolHandler.java:285) at org.jkiss.dbeaver.ext.mysql.tasks.MySQLNativeToolHandler.doExecute(MySQLNativeToolHandler.java:47) at org.jkiss.dbeaver.tasks.nativetool.AbstractNativeToolHandler.lambda$executeTask$0(AbstractNativeToolHandler.java:88) at org.jkiss.dbeaver.runtime.RunnableContextDelegate.lambda$run$0(RunnableContextDelegate.java:39) at org.eclipse.jface.operation.ModalContext$ModalContextThread.run(ModalContext.java:123) I was unable to get the DBeaver export/dump to work. These were my configuration settings. I don't understand why the DBeaver backup failed. I executed a mysqldump using MySQL Workbench for the source database, and it worked just fine. I won't always have Workbench available as it is no longer certified after MySQL v8.0. I need to get DBeaver exports/dumps working. Thanks for your assistance. ### DBeaver Version Community Edition Version 26.1.2.202607051737 ### Operating System Windows 11 Enterprise ### Database and driver ### Steps to reproduce 1. I connected to the MySQL instance. 2. I selected (right clicked) the source database to be copied. 3. I selected [**Tools**] > **Dump database** 4. I validated the database object and selected Next 5. I established the export configuration settings. 6. I selected **Start** ### Additional context I attempted to locate the DBeaver log in the %APPDATA%/Roaming/DBeaerData/workspace6/.metadata folder, but the files do not equate with the timestamp of my failed export.

title: Support custom auth model in Custom (CSV/XML) connection import
author: weih-kahoot
body:
**Feature request**

The Custom connection import wizard (File → Import → Custom) reads connections from CSV/XML files, mapping columns such as `name`, `host`, `port`, `database`, `user`, `password`. However, there is no way to specify the **authentication model** per connection, so every imported connection falls back to the driver's default (Database Native).

This means connections that should use a non-default auth model — e.g. PostgreSQL **PgPass** (`postgres_pgpass`), or Kerberos — cannot be set up through CSV/XML import and must be reconfigured by hand afterwards.

**Proposal:** support an optional `authModelId` column/attribute in the Custom import format, applied to each imported connection when present. Other importers (e.g. SQLWorkbench) already set `authModelId` internally; this simply exposes it in the Custom format.

title: Wrong "Invalid column reference" warning in PostgreSQL function/procedure parameter declaration
author: 0MG-DEN
body:
### Description

When using both `OR REPLACE` and `IN` in PostgreSQL function/procedure declaration, DBeaver shows "Invalid column reference" warning at the parameter's datatype. This warning shows up only when both `OR REPLACE` and `IN` are present - it does not occur when either is omitted.

### DBeaver Version

DBeaver Community Edition 26.1.2.202607051737

### Operating System

_No response_

### Database and driver

1. Database name and version: PostgreSQL 13.12, PostgreSQL 18.4
2. Driver name and version: PostgreSQL JDBC Driver 42.7.11

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new script file.
2. Copy and paste following function/procedure declarations:
`create or replace function  test_func(in x int4) returns int4 language sql as $$ select x; $$;`
`create or replace procedure test_proc(in x int4) language sql as $$ $$;`
3. Observe the warning at `int4`.
4. Remove any of `or replace` or `in` - warning disappears.

### Additional context

Output of `version()` for both PostgreSQL installations:
`PostgreSQL 13.12, compiled by Visual C++ build 1925, 64-bit`
`PostgreSQL 18.4 on x86_64-windows, compiled by msvc-19.41.34120, 64-bit`

title: GBase 8s / Informix: "Routine (read_defaultstr) can not be resolved" when opening table Properties if the routine is missing
author: wjy940520
body:
### Description

Description:
When connecting DBeaver to a GBase 8s V8.8 database via the official gbasedbtjdbc driver,
querying table data works fine, but opening a table's Properties / Columns tab fails with:
SQL error [IX000]: Routine (read_defaultstr) can not be resolved.
  Routine (read_defaultstr) can not be resolved.
    SQLException
Steps to reproduce:
Create a GBase 8s (Informix-compatible) connection in DBeaver using gbasedbtjdbc (tested 3.3.0 / 3.5.1 / 3.6.3).
Connect successfully; plain SELECT on tables works.
Right-click a table → Properties → switch to the Columns tab (column metadata load).
The error is thrown and the Properties panel cannot load.
Environment:
DBeaver version: 26.1.2 (latest)
Database: GBase 8s V8.8
JDBC driver: gbasedbtjdbc_3.3.0, gbasedbtjdbc_3.5.1_3X2_1, gbasedbtjdbc_3.6.3 (all reproduce)
Error origin in log: org.jkiss.dbeaver.model (DBeaver's own metadata layer)
Root cause:
DBeaver's Informix/GBase metadata adapter hard-codes a call to the read_defaultstr() stored procedure
when reading column defaults. However, read_defaultstr is not guaranteed to exist in every GBase 8s V8.8 instance.
Evidence:
SELECT procname FROM sysprocedures WHERE LOWER(procname)='read_defaultstr'; returns 0 rows in the connected DB.
GBase's own native client tools (dbaccess / GBaseDataStudio native path) open the same table's properties
without any error, because they read syscolumns.default directly and never call read_defaultstr.
Toggling DBeaver metadata options (Generate DLLs with extra info, Do not read tables information, etc.) does not help.
The database cannot be modified (security policy), so creating the routine server-side is not an option.
Expected behavior:
DBeaver should not hard-fail the entire Properties panel when read_defaultstr is unresolvable.
It should either:
Read the column default directly from syscolumns.default (consistent with GBase's own tooling), or
Wrap the read_defaultstr call in a try/catch and gracefully degrade (show empty/default value) instead of blocking the whole panel.
Suggested fix:
Make the column-default resolution in the Informix/GBase meta model resilient:
Prefer reading syscolumns.default directly, OR
Fall back to it / leave the default blank when read_defaultstr cannot be resolved,
rather than surfacing IX000 -674 and preventing table properties from opening.
Workarounds tried (none sufficient):
Switched JDBC driver versions (3.3.0 → 3.5.1 → 3.6.3): same error.
Disabled DBeaver metadata options: no effect.
Cannot create the routine on the server (security policy).
Current usable workaround: query syscolumns.default directly via SQL — but this should not be necessary.

### DBeaver Version

26.1.2

### Operating System

window 11

### Database and driver

gbase 8s  V8.8

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: ClickHouse: Support JSON/Value viewer for Map columns with String keys
author: julian-kofler
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Just like native `JSON` columns, ClickHouse `Map` columns with string keys are currently displayed as a flattened, raw string (e.g., `{env=prod, status=200}`). This makes telemetry data completely unreadable in the data grid. This is a massive headache when working with default OpenTelemetry/ClickStack schemas, which heavily rely on columns like:
`SpanAttributes Map(LowCardinality(String), String) CODEC(ZSTD(1))`

**Describe the solution you'd like**
When a ClickHouse `Map` uses `String` or `LowCardinality(String)` as the key type, DBeaver should map it to the structured JSON/Text viewer in the Value panel (similar to the approach in #41315). This would allow users to cleanly read the attributes.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually casting columns in every query using `cast(SpanAttributes, 'JSON')`. However, this is tedious and doesn't help when browsing the table directly via the Data tab.

**Additional context**
This is a direct parallel to **#41315**. As highlighted in the official ClickHouse documentation on [Map vs JSON type for ClickStack](https://clickhouse.com/docs/use-cases/observability/clickstack/ingesting-data/schema/map-vs-json), the default and recommended schema for handling typical high-throughput observability workloads continues to use `Map(LowCardinality(String), String)`. Because the standard setup relies so heavily on this data type for attributes, improving the UX for string-keyed `Map` columns in DBeaver remains essential even with native `JSON` support available.

title: VECTOR type support Oracle DB
author: albertopasqualetto
body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** Vector type support for Oracle DB queries. By default it does not show anything but an error. **Describe the solution you'd like** Have the type supported out of the box and fully explorable. I have seen there is a specifc visualization for VECTOR type in other PRs (#23391) **Describe alternatives you've considered** Setting manually driver property `oracle.jdbc.vectorDefaultGetObjectType=double[]`, but at least it should be set by default. In this way It shown in the column row, but in the Value panel it is showed as object address (I suppose), so the support is not complete. **Additional context** When selecting a column with a vector type, there is the error: ``` org.jkiss.dbeaver.model.exec.DBCException: SQL Error [17004] [99999]: ORA-17004: Invalid column type: JDBC 4.3 does not specify a default conversion for VECTOR. A default conversion may be configured using the "oracle.jdbc.vectorDefaultGetObjectType" connection property https://docs.oracle.com/error-help/db/ora-17004/ ``` Oracle's relative documentation: https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/26/jjdbc/working-with-vectors.html

title: SQL Server semantic analyzer incorrectly validates datepart arguments as column references
author: 4MaxR
body: ### Description ### DBeaver Version - DBeaver Community 26.1.2 - Release date: July 6, 2026 ### Database - Microsoft SQL Server ### Steps to reproduce 1. Enable: - SQL Editor → Query Analysis → Enable semantic analysis - SQL Editor → Query Analysis → Read database table/columns for semantic analysis 2. Execute the following query: ```sql SELECT DATENAME(month, o.CreationTime) FROM Sales.Orders o; ``` ### Actual behavior The editor underlines `month` and shows: ``` Invalid column reference ``` The query executes successfully and SQL Server returns the correct result. ### Expected behavior The datepart argument (`month`, `day`, `weekday`, `dw`, etc.) should be recognized as a valid SQL Server datepart keyword and should not be validated as a column reference. ### Additional information The issue only occurs when the second argument is a table column. For example: ```sql SELECT DATENAME(month, GETDATE()); ``` produces **no warning**. But: ```sql SELECT DATENAME(month, o.CreationTime) FROM Sales.Orders o; ``` produces the false warning. Disabling **"Read database table/columns for semantic analysis"** removes the warning. ### DBeaver Version Community Edition 26.1.2 ### Operating System Windows 11 24H2 (64-bit) ### Database and driver Database: Microsoft SQL Server Connection: Windows Authentication Driver: MS SQL Server / SQL Server ### Steps to reproduce 1. Enable: - SQL Editor → Query Analysis → Enable semantic analysis - SQL Editor → Query Analysis → Read database table/columns for semantic analysis 2. Execute: ```sql SELECT DATENAME(month, o.CreationTime) FROM Sales.Orders o; ``` 3. Observe that `month` is underlined as an invalid column reference. ### Additional context The issue affects multiple SQL Server functions that accept a datepart argument, including: ```sql DATENAME(WEEKDAY, order_date) DATEPART(DAY, order_date) DATETRUNC(DAY, order_date) ``` All queries execute successfully in SQL Server. The false validation only occurs when the date expression is a table column. For example: ```sql SELECT DATENAME(month, GETDATE()); ``` does not produce the warning. Disabling **"Read database table/columns for semantic analysis"** removes the false warning.
